text:
Once upon a time, there was a garden gnome named Gnat. Gnat lived in a quiet corner of an unusual garden filled with talking flowers, a musical oak tree, and a library of ancient tomes never borrowed.

One blustery autumn day, Gnat decided to go on an adventure in search of the most fantastic flower in the garden. He trailed a tiny feather boa along the winding pathways until he stumbled upon a field illuminated by the soft glow of moonlight.

There stood a giant moonflower—the biggest thing Gnat had ever seen. Its petals shimmered like silvery moonlight. Gnat sat down to admire it when he heard a soft voice.

"I've never seen anyone enjoy moonflowers quite like you."

Gnat looked up to see a fairy, as tiny as a feather on Gnat's own head.

"Oh dear! I wasn't trying to bother you," Gnat said hastily. "Just admiring the view."

"I admire your courage," she whispered. "
